title: The Unconscious Hub
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Yürk’s Dreamscape / The Unconscious Hub}} {{Class 0|security=Unstable|entity=Mild Aftereffects|color-h=0|color-s=0%|color-l=60%}} '''Yürk’s Dreamscape''', named after the discoverer Yürk Wean in 1987, informally nicknamed the '''Unconscious Hub''', is an anomalous sub-level of [[The Hub]] one can only access by having a dream in it. Once you enter it, you immediately become lucid and aware of your surroundings. ==
Description
== '''The Unconscious Hub''' serves as a small hub-space to give entrance to a few random normal levels, rarely accessed levels (typically undocumented within the database), and to 3 levels that are only accessible via '''The Unconscious Hub'''. The sub-level is described to be a small room consisting of between 5 to 14 wooden labelled doors. However, since the room is still affected by any of the elements of the dream, and one had gained lucidity, dreams of the level can be greatly unstable to those that do not know how to handle lucidity correctly, and typically only lasts for a few seconds. Another outcome is that one may slip back into the dream, losing lucidity, or being transitioned into a regular lucid dream. Effects have been observed from continuous dreaming of the sub-level, causing sleep disturbance - some report having significant insomnia while others having sustained sleeps exceeding beyond 14 hours, homeostasis completely shifted backwards, increased daydreaming, and in-dream disruption, causing consciousness within a void state before a dream, or prolonging of dreams beyond its limits. These effects can last from 3 days to 2 weeks. There is a chance of multiple people being in '''The Unconscious Hub''' at concurrence, though causing a few differences in their dreams. Most of the time, this causes a prolonged dream, and has the potential to be more unstable. For instance, the '''Tripper Effect'''. How it works is that each person can only see their own dream and is unable to see from other people’s dreams. Although, if each dream is complex enough in nature, dreams may fuse with one another, and it is this effect which causes more amplified after effects of '''The Unconscious Hub''' (refer to the previous paragraph). Although, despite instability, the affected individuals are likely still kept in the dream, and unstable dreams are often overwhelming and overstimulating. Dreams are estimated to cease in 22 to 30 minutes unless one goes through a door to another level. {| style="float: {{{align|right}}}; margin: 0.1em; border-spacing: 0.3em 0; border-width: 5px;" |+ Level Data |- | style="font-size: 85%; text-align: center; background: black;" | Dependent Rarity/Skill-Based Rarity |- | style="font-size: 85%; text-align: center; background: black;" | Discovered by Yürk Wean |- | style="font-size: 85%; text-align: center; background: black;" | Discovered 38 years ago (1987, March 7th) |- | style="font-size: 85%; text-align: center; background: black;" | Documented by Jaren Irea and Seth Dac |- | style="font-size: 85%; text-align: center; background: black;" | Documented 20 years ago (2005, December 8th / Revised 2006 July 10th) |- | style="font-size: 85%; text-align: center; background: black;" | Mild aftereffcts |- | style="font-size: 85%; text-align: center; background: black;" | Oddball/Anomalous Sub-level |} If one wanted to sustain a stay here or wanted a guaranteed entrance to the level, one should do so using a lucid dream. Even so, the heightened lucidity within this sublevel causes short stays even if one is experienced in lucid dreaming. At most, stays last from 5 to 10 minutes. Upon entering a door, however, there is a small chance one will be transported to the level but however experience it in their dream. When they are in this state, dreams are unrealistically sustained, one typically being stuck within their dream from 2 to 3 days. In addition, external disturbances seem to not wake up the person. ==
Addendum
== The following are recovered diary entries from Yürk Wean during the discovery and exploration of the '''Unconscious Hub'''. It came to me quietly during a silent wander in my lucid dream. The scene was an endless calm ocean, my feet slightly submerged, and the focused fog moving with the breeze in the distance. The environment then transitioned smoothly into this small chamber. I didn’t get a good look this night however. I felt myself becoming more conscious, then waking up in a few seconds. This is my second time experiencing such a location. Does that place mean something? I then woke up to [[The Hub]]’s presence.
It came again but I was normally dreaming this time. The dream was about floating controllably throughout the universe, and when I was at lightspeed, all the stars and clusters blurred into an iridescent ever-surronding globe of light around me, now in a still picture. I was in the room again, and this environment became the material of the surroundings. I took a glance, seeing 8 wooden doors being labelled with something that I couldn’t quite read. Maybe I don’t remember. This dream only lasted half a minute and I found it hard to focus. It appears that consciousness is increased heavily in this room, hence is why every visit there always is short. Even for me, who is experienced in lucid dreaming. I will see if I could enter again tonight.
I experienced a false-awakening, walking around [[The Hub]] before acutely becoming conscious. I summoned a door that would let me into the room, and it didn’t open. No matter how much doors I summoned, it didn’t budge. However, whilst grounding myself within the lucid dream, I felt something in my presence that made me face to a new wall along with a bland wooden door that was center behind me. I did not think of this door, additionally, and when I opened it I was welcomed once again to the room. This time, its appearance took mimicry of [[The Hub]], and had 6 doors. The door labels read level numbers I had forgotten now. I spent a minute before waking up and experiencing brief sleep paralysis. The appearance is never consistent, and the doors quantity may not be consistent either. There seems to be something preventing me from remembering what the door labels completely say. Next dream, I will find out what’s its purpose is.
I seemed to be conscious in the void this time. I dreamt nothing and was aware? After a minute, I felt like my eyes were gradually being forced awake in this dream. A blurry concrete wall along with a wooden door was starting to fall into my vision. The only choice I had left was to open the door in front of me. I was greeted into the silent room, leaving me to focus solely on the doors at hand. This time, there were 14 doors, and I forced the labels to be readable for me. The first door I read was labelled ‘Level 832’. I invited myself in, and was teleported to another level. However, I was still in the dream. The environment was an endless array of bridges, built from endlessly detailed brutalist architecture. Even though the chamber made me fully aware, I felt like I was truly awake in here. The only way I could tell I was still in the dream in the moment was… I’m not sure. But even after an hour of endless searching and wandering, and along with my questioned mind to ponder my true goal here, I never awoken. My inner harmony centered to consonance when I saw the brown wooden door. I was then sent back to the room, and ungrounded myself by thinking about my body still sleeping, to then be finally awaken. Never have I came across such mistake… such fragmentation in my unconscious world. How is it so that I came to wake to nothing? To be paralysed in the void? How was it so that the mind can entrap itself like as if it had woken up? How is it so…?
I spent 3 days getting to [[M.E.G.|M.E.Gs]] Base, ‘First Light’ to tell my findings. I know this wouldn’t do much anyway, but I felt like I had the need to publicise this. I can’t investigate all by myself after discovering the intricacies of what I should call now '''The Unconscious Hub'''. I need at least one person to be invested into this location. If I’m center, and one ends up into that room, even for a few seconds, I would have my proof. I’m sure that this isn’t a result of a figment of my imagination, or the effect of some disorder; but, I will not go back there again deliberately. I can not afford to get lost once again. Those who explore shall be careful, to not be left behind by the compacts of their own mind. If only if it was their mind at work here. ==
Entrances and Exits
== ===
Entrances
=== *The only entrance is for you to dream in [[The Hub]], to have a random chance for it to shift into '''The Unconscious Hub'''. *It is also possible to enter directly via a lucid dream. ===
Exits
=== *Waking up from the dream to end up in [[The Hub]] *Entering a door with an attributed level to enter. **[[Level 30]] **[[Level 68]] **[[Level 79]] **[[Level 99]] **2 random level slots (though labelled) **4 random undocumented/rare level slots (though labelled)
